data.title: Novo Nordisk Secures FDA Approval For Higher-Dose Wegovy
data.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted approval for a higher-dose version of Novo Nordisk A/S' popular weight-loss drug, Wegovy. This new Wegovy HD (semaglutide) injection at 7.2 mg aims to assist adults dealing with obesity who have previously adapted well to the 2.4 mg dose and are seeking further weight reduction. The drug is recommended to be utilized alongside a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity. This approval was facilitated through the Commissioner's National Priority Voucher (CNPV) expedited program. The decision was supported by data from the STEP UP clinical program, particularly a 72-week study involving 1,407 obese adults. The expansion of Novo Nordisk's treatment portfolio comes at a time when their shares are under pressure in the market.

data.title: Consumer Watchdog Report Shows, Uber's New R2 Rivian Robotaxi Fleet Won't Be Accountable To Drivers and Pedestrians They Injure And Kill Under Uber's 2026 "License To Kill" CA Ballot Initiative
data.description: In Los Angeles on March 19, 2026, Uber revealed a collaboration to deploy 50,000 Rivian Robotaxis, which will also operate in San Francisco. A non-profit organization, Consumer Watchdog, released a recent report highlighting a ballot measure planned by Uber for 2026 in California.

data.title: Gas prices spike due to Persian Gulf crisis; if oil stays above $100/barrel, inflation may rise -Ben Casselman #economy
data.description: In a recent analysis by our chief economics correspondent, Ben Casselman, the impact of the oil crisis in the Persian Gulf on gasoline prices was examined. With the price of oil hovering above $100 per barrel, there are concerns about its effect on inflation. Casselman explores the potential consequences of this ongoing situation on consumer costs and economic stability.

data.title: Fed chair intends to delay departure, sparking possible clash with Trump over future leadership at central bank.
data.description: The current chair of the Federal Reserve has signaled his intention to remain in his position for the foreseeable future, potentially sparking a conflict with President Trump. The potential clash arises as the chair's decision could impact the President's plans for the central bank.
Investors should not expect any support from the Fed during this crisis.

data.title: Chat with AI instead of son; Silicon Valley coders experiment with tech, raising concerns about bonding time.
data.description: Young programmers in Silicon Valley are unleashing their creativity with the latest technology, but some are concerned they are not dedicating sufficient time to it.

data.title: Over 70 allies, donors, and fraud convicts pardoned by Trump during his presidency, despite promises to crack down on fraud.
data.description: During his two terms in office, President Trump has issued over 70 acts of clemency to individuals including allies, supporters, and donors who were convicted in cases related to fraud.

data.title: Record high gas prices in Japan challenge leader's cost-of-living pledge. Subsidies deployed to ease impact amid long-term risks from Middle East conflict.
data.description: Japan is implementing subsidies to mitigate the effects of rising fuel prices, as it grapples with the economic uncertainties stemming from the Middle East conflict.

data.title: Inflation poses threat to Europe and Asia's growth post-2022 energy crisis due to Iran war's economic impact.
data.description: The increasing inflation rates and their impact on economic growth have become a significant worry for nations that are still recovering from the 2022 energy crisis. This issue is particularly relevant given the current inflation rate of X%, resulting in various challenges for sustainable development and economic stability. The lingering effects of the energy crisis continue to influence policy-making and future projections, underscoring the importance of proactive measures to address these intertwined issues effectively.

data.title: Ozempic set to become generic in India, China, Canada with Novo Nordisk losing patent protection, allowing for cheaper alternatives.
data.description: Patent protection for Novo Nordisk's popular weight loss drug is nearing expiration in India, China, and various other countries. This development will allow for more affordable generic versions of the drug to enter the market, posing a potential threat to the company's current market share.

data.title: Powell to stay as Fed Chair until successor confirmed. He vows to remain until investigation on handling of renovations is completed.
data.description: Jerome H. Powell, the current head of the central bank, has announced that he will not step down from his position at the Federal Reserve until the completion of a criminal investigation regarding his management of renovation projects.

data.title: Guggenheim Investments Again Ranked Among Top Taxable Bond Fund Families by Barron's
data.description: Company achieves its fourth top-five ranking since 2020, with three of those years at the top spot.

data.title: New Issue Brief from Rivera: PBM Reform Raises the Bar for Pharmacy Benefit Oversight but Does Not Replace It
data.description: In Columbus, Ohio, Rivera, a pharmacy payment integrity company, released a new issue brief discussing the heightened importance of pharmacy benefit oversight amidst the evolving landscape of PBM reform. Recent governmental actions at both federal and state levels have intensified scrutiny on pharmacy benefit administration, particularly in relation to Medicare Part D, ERISA disclosure standards, and overall PBM practices. These reforms emphasize the need for enhanced monitoring to ensure transparency and accountability within the pharmacy benefits sector, with legislation serving as a critical component but not the sole solution.

data.title: Horizon Quantum, a Leading Quantum Software Infrastructure Company, Closes its Business Combination with dMY Squared
data.description: Horizon Quantum Computing Pte. Ltd. has finalized its merger with dMY Squared Technology Group, Inc., a special purpose acquisition company listed under "DMYY". The software infrastructure for quantum applications provider, based in Singapore and Las Vegas, successfully completed the business combination. With shareholders giving their approval at dMY’s special meeting, the merger marks a significant step for Horizon Quantum.
